revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Shizuoka_Gokoku_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Shizuoka_Gokoku_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{神社| 名称=靜岡縣護國神社| 所在地=静岡県静岡市葵区柚木366| 祭神=静岡県の英霊| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社| 創建=明治32年（1899年）| 例祭=10月22日、23日|}} '''靜岡縣護國神社'''（'''しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ'''）は[[静岡県]][[静岡市]]葵区にある神社である。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]に至る静岡県出身の英霊7万6千余柱を祀る。
社務所の二階は遺品館になっており、戦没者の遺品約4千点が展示されている。 == 沿革 == [[明治32年]][[11月13日]]-「共祭招魂社」として静岡市北番町に創建
[[昭和14年]][[4月1日]]-「靜岡縣護國神社」と改称
[[昭和17年]][[10月8日]]-現在地に移転遷座
[[平成10年]][[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]]-御鎮座百年奉祝大祭斎行
== 主な祭事 == *歳旦祭-[[1月1日]] *節分祭-[[2月3日]] *紀元祭-[[2月11日]] *祈年祭-[[2月17日]] *菖蒲祭-[[5月5日]] *大祓式-[[6月30日]] *万灯みたま祭-[[8月13日]]～[[8月15日|15日]] *例祭-[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] *明治祭-[[11月3日]] *七五三祭-[[11月15日]] *新嘗祭-[[11月23日]] *煤払祭-[[12月13日]] *天長祭-[[12月23日]] *大祓式-[[12月31日]] *除夜祭-[[12月31日]] == 外部リンク == [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja/6003.html 靜岡縣護國神社（静岡県神社庁）] [[Category:静岡県の神社|しすおかけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|しすおかけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|しすおかけんここくしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=靜岡縣護國神社| 画像=[[画像:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|靜岡縣護國神社]]| 所在地=静岡県静岡市葵区柚木366| 祭神=静岡県の英霊 76,219柱| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社| 創建=明治32年（1899年）| 本殿=流造| 例祭=10月22日、23日|}} '''靜岡縣護國神社'''（'''しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ'''）は[[静岡県]][[静岡市]]葵区にある神社である。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]（[[太平洋戦争]]）に至る静岡県出身の英霊7万6千余柱を祀る。
社務所の二階は遺品館になっており、戦没者の遺品約4千点が展示されている。 == 沿革 == *[[1899年|明治32年]][[11月13日]]-「共祭招魂社」として静岡市北番町に創建 *[[1939年|昭和14年]][[4月1日]]-「靜岡縣護國神社」と改称 *[[1942年|昭和17年]][[10月8日]]-現在地に移転遷座 *[[1946年|昭和21年]]-「靜霊神社」と改称。 *[[1953年|昭和28年]]-「靜岡縣護國神社」と復称。 *[[1998年|平成10年]][[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]]-御鎮座百年奉祝大祭斎行 == 主な祭事 == *歳旦祭-[[1月1日]] *節分祭-[[2月3日]] *紀元祭-[[2月11日]] *祈年祭-[[2月17日]] *菖蒲祭-[[5月5日]] *大祓式-[[6月30日]] *万灯みたま祭-[[8月13日]]～[[8月15日|15日]] *例祭-[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] *明治祭-[[11月3日]] *七五三祭-[[11月15日]] *新嘗祭-[[11月23日]] *煤払祭-[[12月13日]] *天長祭-[[12月23日]] *大祓式-[[12月31日]] *除夜祭-[[12月31日]] ==その他== [[1989年]]、静岡市制100周年記念事業として、舞台公演"NINAGAWA[[マクベス (シェイクスピア)|マクベス]]"(演出：[[蜷川幸雄]])が上演された。またこの他にも[[オカリナ]]奏者である[[宗次郎]]のコンサートを開催したことがある。 == 関連項目 == *[[護国神社]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 靜岡縣護國神社（静岡県神社庁）] *[http://www.shizuokagokoku.jp/ 靜岡縣護國神社（オフィシャルサイト）] {{DEFAULTSORT:しすおかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:静岡市の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:葵区]] {{shinto-stub}}

社務所の二階は遺品館になっており、戦没者の遺品約4千点が展示されている。 == 沿革 == *[[1899年|明治32年]][[11月13日]] -「共祭招魂社」として静岡市北番町に創建 *[[1939年|昭和14年]][[4月1日]] -「靜岡縣護國神社」と改称 *[[1942年|昭和17年]][[10月8日]]- 現在地に移転遷座 *[[1946年|昭和21年]] -「靜霊神社」と改称 *[[1952年|昭和27年]]12月 - [[宗教法人法]]による[[宗教法人]]として知事の認証を受け登記 *[[1953年|昭和28年]] -「靜岡縣護國神社」と復称 *[[1998年|平成10年]][[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] - 御鎮座百年奉祝大祭斎行 == 主な祭事 == [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』田中忠雄建築事務所設計、平井工業施工『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』[[1952年]]。]] [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[斎藤寿夫 (政治家)|齊藤寿夫]]誌『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[1952年]]。（左）と『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[石川嘉延]]書『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[1995年]]。（右）]] *歳旦祭-[[1月1日]] *節分祭-[[2月3日]] *紀元祭-[[2月11日]] *祈年祭-[[2月17日]] *菖蒲祭-[[5月5日]] *大祓式-[[6月30日]] *万灯みたま祭-[[8月13日]]～[[8月15日|15日]] *例祭-[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] *明治祭-[[11月3日]] *七五三祭-[[11月15日]] *新嘗祭-[[11月23日]] *煤払祭-[[12月13日]] *天長祭-[[12月23日]] *大祓式-[[12月31日]] *除夜祭-[[12月31日]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[護国神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuokagokoku.jp/ 靜岡縣護國神社（オフィシャルサイト）] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 靜岡縣護國神社（静岡県神社庁）] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすおかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:葵区の歴史]] [[Category:葵区の建築物]]

社務所2階の戦没者遺品館は駐車場に建設した新つつじ会館内に移転、戦没者の遺品や戦時記念品約千点が展示されている。 == 沿革 == *[[1899年|明治32年]][[11月13日]] -「共祭招魂社」として静岡市北番町に創建 *[[1939年|昭和14年]][[4月1日]] -「靜岡縣護國神社」と改称 *[[1942年|昭和17年]][[10月8日]]- 現在地に移転遷座 *[[1946年|昭和21年]] -「靜霊神社」と改称 *[[1952年|昭和27年]]12月 - [[宗教法人法]]による[[宗教法人]]として知事の認証を受け登記完了 *[[1953年|昭和28年]] -「靜岡縣護國神社」と復称 *[[1998年|平成10年]][[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] - 御鎮座百年奉祝大祭斎行 *平成30年10月22日、23日 - 御鎮座百二十年奉祝大祭斎行 == 主な祭事 == [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』田中忠雄建築事務所設計、平井工業施工『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』[[1952年]]。]] [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[斎藤寿夫 (政治家)|齊藤寿夫]]誌『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[1952年]]。（左）と『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[石川嘉延]]書『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[1995年]]。（右）]] *歳旦祭-[[1月1日]] *元始祭‐[[1月1日|1月3日]] *節分祭-[[2月3日]] *紀元祭-[[2月11日]] *祈年祭-[[2月17日]] *天長祭-[[2月23日]] *菖蒲祭-[[5月5日]] *御田植祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|6月]]吉日 *夏越大祓式-[[6月30日]] *万灯みたま祭-[[8月13日]]～[[8月15日|15日]] *英霊顕彰祭-[[8月13日|8月]][[8月15日|15日]] *抜穂祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|10月]]吉日 *収穫奉告祭-抜穂祭同日 *例祭「慰霊大祭」-[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] *明治祭-[[11月3日]] *七五三祭-[[11月15日]] *新嘗祭-[[11月23日]] *御煤払祭-[[12月13日]] *年越大祓式-[[12月31日]] *除夜祭-12月31日 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[護国神社]] *[[静岡陸軍墓地]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuokagokoku.jp/ 靜岡縣護國神社（オフィシャルサイト）] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 靜岡縣護國神社（静岡県神社庁）] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすおかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:葵区の歴史]] [[Category:葵区の建築物]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=靜岡縣護國神社 |画像=[[画像:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|靜岡縣護國神社]] |所在地=静岡県静岡市葵区柚木366 |祭神=静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者 76,232柱 |社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建=明治32年（1899年） |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 59 | 緯度秒 = 15 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 25.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = JP-22 |ラベル位置 = bottom |地図= Japan Shizuoka }} '''靜岡縣護國神社'''（'''しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ'''）は[[静岡県]][[静岡市]]葵区にある神社である。神紋は「丸に桜」である。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]（[[太平洋戦争]]）における静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者7万6千余柱を祀る。 戦時中までは内務省指定護国神社(県社格)であり、戦後は新規設立された神社本庁の包括下となり宗教法人認証を受けてのち昭和28年5月29日に神社本庁における別表神社に加列した。
社務所2階の戦没者遺品館は駐車場に建設移転した新つつじ会館(静岡県遺族会事務局併設)に移設、戦没英霊の遺品や戦時記念品約千点と戦没者遺影(掲出事業篤志協賛)が展示されている。 == 沿革 == *明治10年-静岡浅間神社において官軍兵士の招魂祭を斎行 *駿河伊豆浜松の三地域で招魂祭を斎行 *明治20年代末、一県共祭の招魂社を建設する機運が高まる *[[1899年|明治32年]][[11月13日]]-「共祭招魂社」として静岡市北番町に創建 *[[1939年|昭和14年]][[4月1日]]-「靜岡縣護國神社」と改称 *[[1942年|昭和17年]][[10月8日]]- 現在地に移転遷座 *[[1946年|昭和22年]]-「靜霊神社/しずたまじんじゃ」と改称 *[[1952年|昭和27年]]12月- [[宗教法人法]]による[[宗教法人]]として知事の認証を受け登記完了 *[[1953年|昭和28年]]-「靜岡縣護國神社」と復称 *[[1998年|平成10年]][[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]]- 御鎮座百年奉祝大祭斎行 *平成30年10月22日、23日- 御鎮座百二十年奉祝大祭斎行 == 主な祭事 == [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』田中忠雄建築事務所設計、平井工業施工『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』[[1952年]]。]] [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[斎藤寿夫 (政治家)|齊藤寿夫]]誌『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[1952年]]。（左）と『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[石川嘉延]]書『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[1995年]]。（右）]] *歳旦祭-[[1月1日]] *元始祭‐[[1月1日|1月3日]] *節分祭-[[2月3日]] *紀元祭-[[2月11日]] *祈年祭-[[2月17日]] *天長祭-[[2月23日]] *静岡県陸軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[2月23日|3月10日]] *昭和祭-[[2月23日|4月29日]] *菖蒲祭-[[5月5日]] *静岡県海軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[5月5日|5月27日]] *御田植祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|6月]]吉日 *夏越大祓式(茅の輪くぐり)-[[6月30日]] *七夕祭-[[6月30日|7月1週目]]吉日 *万灯みたま祭-[[8月13日]]～[[8月15日|15日]] *英霊顕彰祭-[[8月13日|8月]][[8月15日|15日]] *抜穂祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|10月]]吉日 *収穫奉告祭-抜穂祭同日 *霊璽奉安祭(合祀祭)-10月21日 ※合祀ある時のみ *例祭「慰霊大祭」-[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] *明治祭-[[11月3日]] *創立記念祭-[[11月15日|11月13日]] *七五三祭-[[11月15日]] *新嘗祭-[[11月23日]] *御煤払祭-[[12月13日]] *年越大祓式-[[12月31日]] *除夜祭-12月31日 *月始祭-毎月1日 ※元日は歳旦祭 *月次祭-毎月13日 ※11月は創立記念祭 *命日祭-毎月数回、及び祥月命日に随時執行 *各部隊慰霊祭-恒例日 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[護国神社]] *[[静岡陸軍墓地]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuokagokoku.jp/ 靜岡縣護國神社（オフィシャルサイト）] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 靜岡縣護國神社（静岡県神社庁）] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすおかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:葵区の歴史]] [[Category:葵区の建築物]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=靜岡縣護國神社 |画像=[[画像:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|靜岡縣護國神社]] |所在地=静岡県静岡市葵区柚木366 |祭神=静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者 76,232柱 |社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建=明治32年（1899年） |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 59 | 緯度秒 = 15 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 25.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = JP-22 |ラベル位置 = bottom |地図= Japan Shizuoka }} '''参拝は社殿6時開門から17時閉門(春夏季18時)※閉門時も自由参拝可能≒24時間営業''' '''社務所は7時半から16時半頃、祈祷9時から16時半頃''' '''靜岡縣護國神社'''（'''しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ'''）は[[静岡県]][[静岡市]]葵区に所在する神社である。神紋は「丸に桜」。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]（[[太平洋戦争]]）における静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者7万6千余柱を祀る。 戦時中までは内務省指定護国神社(県社格)であり、戦後は新規設立された神社本庁の包括下となり宗教法人認証を受けてのち昭和28年5月29日に神社本庁における別表神社に加列した。

社務所2階の戦没者遺品館は駐車場に建設移転した新つつじ会館(静岡県遺族会事務局併設)に移設、戦没英霊の遺品や戦時記念品約千点と戦没者遺影(掲出事業篤志協賛)が展示されている。 == 沿革 == *明治10年-静岡浅間神社において官軍兵士の招魂祭を斎行 *駿河伊豆浜松の三地域で招魂祭を斎行 *明治20年代末、一県共祭の招魂社を建設する機運が高まる *[[1899年|明治32年]][[11月13日]]-「共祭招魂社」として静岡市北番町に創建 *[[1939年|昭和14年]][[4月1日]]-「靜岡縣護國神社」と改称 *[[1942年|昭和17年]][[10月8日]]- 現在地に移転遷座 *[[1946年|昭和22年]]-「靜霊神社/しずたまじんじゃ」と改称 *[[1952年|昭和27年]]12月- [[宗教法人法]]による[[宗教法人]]として知事の認証を受け登記完了 *[[1953年|昭和28年]]-「靜岡縣護國神社」と復称 *[[1998年|平成10年]][[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]]- 御鎮座百年奉祝大祭斎行 *平成30年10月22日、23日- 御鎮座百二十年奉祝大祭斎行 == 主な祭事 == [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』田中忠雄建築事務所設計、平井工業施工『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』[[1952年]]。]] [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[斎藤寿夫 (政治家)|齊藤寿夫]]誌『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[1952年]]。（左）と『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[石川嘉延]]書『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[1995年]]。（右）]] *歳旦祭-[[1月1日]] *元始祭‐[[1月1日|1月3日]] *節分祭-[[2月3日]] *紀元祭-[[2月11日]] *祈年祭-[[2月17日]] *天長祭-[[2月23日]] *静岡県陸軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[2月23日|3月10日]] *昭和祭-[[2月23日|4月29日]] *菖蒲祭-[[5月5日]] *静岡県海軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[5月5日|5月27日]] *御田植祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|6月]]吉日 *夏越大祓式(茅の輪くぐり)-[[6月30日]] *七夕祭-[[6月30日|7月1週目]]吉日 *万灯みたま祭-[[8月13日]]～[[8月15日|15日]] *英霊顕彰祭-[[8月13日|8月]][[8月15日|15日]] *抜穂祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|10月]]吉日 *収穫奉告祭-抜穂祭同日 *霊璽奉安祭(合祀祭)-10月21日 ※合祀ある時のみ *例祭「慰霊大祭」-[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] *明治祭-[[11月3日]] *創立記念祭-[[11月15日|11月13日]] *七五三祭-[[11月15日]] *新嘗祭-[[11月23日]] *御煤払祭-[[12月13日]] *年越大祓式-[[12月31日]] *除夜祭-12月31日 *月始祭-毎月1日 ※元日は歳旦祭 *月次祭-毎月13日 ※11月は創立記念祭 *命日祭-毎月数回、及び祥月命日に随時執行 *各部隊慰霊祭-恒例日 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[護国神社]] *[[静岡陸軍墓地]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuokagokoku.jp/ 靜岡縣護國神社（オフィシャルサイト）] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 靜岡縣護國神社（静岡県神社庁）] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすおかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:葵区の歴史]] [[Category:葵区の建築物]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=靜岡縣護國神社 |画像=[[画像:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|靜岡縣護國神社]] |所在地=静岡県静岡市葵区柚木366 |祭神=静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者 76,232柱 |社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建=明治32年（1899年） |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 59 | 緯度秒 = 15 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 25.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = JP-22 |ラベル位置 = bottom |地図= Japan Shizuoka }} 参拝は社殿6時開門から17時閉門(春夏季18時)まで 　※閉門時も少し離れた位置で自由参拝可 ≒24時間営業 　※社務所の対応時間は公式ウェブページに記載 '''靜岡縣護國神社'''（'''しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ'''）は[[静岡県]][[静岡市]]葵区に所在する神社である。神紋は「丸に桜」。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]（[[太平洋戦争]]）における静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者7万6千余柱を祀る。 戦時中までは内務省指定護国神社(県社格)であり、戦後は新規設立された神社本庁の包括下となり個別の宗教法人認証を受けて昭和28年5月29日に神社本庁における別表神社に加列(取扱の類別)された。

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=靜岡縣護國神社 |画像=[[画像:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|靜岡縣護國神社]] |所在地=静岡県静岡市葵区柚木366 |祭神=静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者 76,232柱 |社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建=明治32年（1899年） |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 59 | 緯度秒 = 15 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 25.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = JP-22 |ラベル位置 = bottom |地図= Japan Shizuoka }} 参拝は社殿6時開門から17時閉門(春夏季18時)まで 　※閉門しても少し離れた位置で自由参拝可 ≒24時間営業(この表記しかできないため) 　※社務所の業務対応時間(＝入店可能時間)は公式ウェブページにも詳述 '''靜岡縣護國神社'''（'''しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ'''）は[[静岡県]][[静岡市]]葵区に所在する神社である。神紋は「丸に桜」。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]（[[太平洋戦争]]）における静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者7万6千余柱を祀る。 戦時中までは内務省指定護国神社(県社格)であり、戦後は新規設立された神社本庁の包括下となり個別の宗教法人認証を受けて昭和28年5月29日に神社本庁における別表神社に加列(取扱の類別)された。

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=靜岡縣護國神社 |画像=[[画像:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|靜岡縣護國神社]] |所在地=静岡県静岡市葵区柚木366 |祭神=静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者 76,232柱 |社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建=明治32年（1899年） |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 59 | 緯度秒 = 15 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 25.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = JP-22 |ラベル位置 = bottom |地図= Japan Shizuoka }} 一般的な参拝は社殿6時開門から17時閉門(春夏季18時閉門)まで 　※閉門後は少し離れた位置で参拝可 ≒24時間営業(ビジネスプロフィールはこの表記しかできないため) 　※社務所の業務対応時間(＝入店可能時間)は公式ウェブページに詳述 '''靜岡縣護國神社'''（'''しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ'''）は[[静岡県]][[静岡市]]葵区に所在する神社である。神紋は「丸に桜」。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]（[[太平洋戦争]]）における静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者7万6千余柱を祀る。 戦時中までは内務省指定護国神社(県社格)であり、戦後は新規設立された神社本庁の包括下となり個別の宗教法人認証を受けて昭和28年5月29日に神社本庁における別表神社に加列(取扱の類別)された。
社務所2階の戦没者遺品館は駐車場に建設移転した新つつじ会館(静岡県遺族会事務局併設)に移設、戦没英霊の遺品や戦時記念品約千点と戦没者遺影(掲出事業篤志協賛)が展示されている。 == 沿革 == *明治10年-静岡浅間神社において官軍兵士戦没者の招魂祭(含、慰霊)を斎行 *駿河伊豆浜松の三地域で戦没者招魂祭(含、慰霊)を斎行 *明治20年代末、一県共祭の招魂社を建設する機運が高まる *[[1899年|明治32年]][[11月13日]]-「共祭招魂社」として静岡市北番町に創建 *[[1939年|昭和14年]][[4月1日]]-内務省指定「靜岡縣護國神社」と改称 *[[1942年|昭和17年]][[10月8日]]- 旧境内地(市内中心部)の狭隘さにより現在地を選定して移転遷座 *[[1946年|昭和22年]]-戦後の世情動静に鑑み「靜霊神社/しずたまじんじゃ」と改称 *[[1952年|昭和27年]]12月- [[宗教法人法]]による[[宗教法人]]として知事の認証を受け登記完了 *[[1953年|昭和28年]]-「靜岡縣護國神社」と復称、所属の神社本庁にて別表神社に加列 *昭和53年-旧社務所老朽化と狭小により社務所新築、竣工 *[[1998年|平成10年]][[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]]- 御鎮座百年奉祝大祭を斎行 *平成30年10月22日、23日- 御鎮座百二十年奉祝大祭を斎行 == 主な祭事 == [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』田中忠雄建築事務所設計、平井工業施工『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』[[1952年]]。]] [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[斎藤寿夫 (政治家)|齊藤寿夫]]誌『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[1952年]]。（左）と『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[石川嘉延]]書『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[1995年]]。（右）]] *歳旦祭-[[1月1日]] *元始祭‐[[1月1日|1月3日]] *節分祭-[[2月3日]] *紀元祭-[[2月11日]] *祈年祭-[[2月17日]] *天長祭-[[2月23日]] *静岡県陸軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[2月23日|3月10日]] *昭和祭-[[2月23日|4月29日]] *菖蒲祭-[[5月5日]] *静岡県海軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[5月5日|5月27日]] *御田植祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|6月]]吉日 *夏越大祓式(茅の輪くぐり)-[[6月30日]] *七夕祭-[[6月30日|7月1週目]]吉日 *万灯みたま祭-[[8月13日]]～[[8月15日|15日]] *英霊顕彰祭-[[8月13日|8月]][[8月15日|15日]] *抜穂祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|10月]]吉日 *収穫奉告祭-抜穂祭同日 *霊璽奉安祭(合祀祭)-10月21日 ※合祀ある時のみ *例祭「慰霊大祭」-[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] *明治祭-[[11月3日]] *創立記念祭-[[11月15日|11月13日]] *七五三祭-[[11月15日]] *新嘗祭-[[11月23日]] *御煤払祭-[[12月13日]] *年越大祓式-[[12月31日]] *除夜祭-12月31日 *月始祭-毎月1日 ※元日は歳旦祭 *月次祭-毎月13日 ※11月は創立記念祭 *命日祭-毎月数回、及び祥月命日に随時執行 *各部隊慰霊祭-恒例日 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[護国神社]] *[[静岡陸軍墓地]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuokagokoku.jp/ 靜岡縣護國神社（オフィシャルサイト）] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 靜岡縣護國神社（静岡県神社庁）] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=靜岡縣護國神社 |画像=[[画像:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|靜岡縣護國神社]] |所在地=静岡県静岡市葵区柚木366 |祭神=静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者 76,233柱 |社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建=明治32年（1899年） |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 59 | 緯度秒 = 15 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 25.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = JP-22 |ラベル位置 = bottom |地図= Japan Shizuoka }} 一般的な参拝は社殿6時開門から17時閉門(春夏季18時閉門)まで 　※閉門後は少し離れた位置で参拝可 ≒24時間営業(ビジネスプロフィールはこの表記しかできないため) 　※社務所の業務対応時間(＝入店可能時間)は公式ウェブページに詳述 '''靜岡縣護國神社'''（'''しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ'''）は[[静岡県]][[静岡市]]葵区に所在する神社である。神紋は「丸に桜」。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]（[[太平洋戦争]]）における静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者7万6千余柱を祀る。 戦時中までは内務省指定護国神社(県社格)であり、国(官)と神社が分離した戦後は新規設立された神社本庁の包括下となり個別の宗教法人認証を受け、昭和28年5月29日に神社本庁における別表神社に加列(取扱の類別)した。
社務所2階の戦没者遺品館は駐車場に建設移転した新つつじ会館(静岡県遺族会事務局併設)に移設、戦没英霊の遺品や戦時記念品約千点と戦没者遺影(掲出事業篤志協賛)を展示している。 == 沿革 == *明治10年-静岡浅間神社(国幣小社、駿河総社)にて官軍兵士戦没者の招魂祭(含、慰霊)が斎行、同神社の社殿群一隅で祭祀継続 *駿河伊豆浜松の三地域で個別に戦没者招魂祭(含、慰霊)が斎行されていた *明治20年代末、一県一社の招魂社(維新志士死者、戦没者慰霊の社)を建設する機運が高まる *[[1899年|明治32年]][[11月13日]]-官民一体で共に祭る「共祭招魂社」として静岡市北番町に創建 *[[1939年|昭和14年]][[4月1日]]-内務省指定「靜岡縣護國神社」と改称 *[[1942年|昭和17年]][[10月8日]]- *例祭「慰霊大祭」-[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] *明治祭-[[11月3日]] *創立記念祭-[[11月15日|11月13日]] *七五三祭-[[11月15日]] *新嘗祭-[[11月23日]] *御煤払祭-[[12月13日]] *年越大祓式-[[12月31日]] *除夜祭-12月31日 *月始祭-毎月1日 ※元日は歳旦祭 *月次祭-毎月13日 ※11月は創立記念祭 [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 靜岡縣護國神社（静岡県神社庁）] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすおかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:葵区の歴史]] [[Category:葵区の建築物]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=靜岡縣護國神社 |画像=[[画像:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|靜岡縣護國神社]] |所在地=静岡県静岡市葵区柚木366 |祭神=静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者 76,233柱 |社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建=明治32年（1899年） |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 59 | 緯度秒 = 15 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 25.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = JP-22 |ラベル位置 = bottom |地図= Japan Shizuoka }} 一般的な参拝は社殿6時開門から17時閉門(春夏季18時閉門)まで 　※閉門後は少し離れた位置で参拝可 ≒24時間営業(ビジネスプロフィールはこの表記しかできないため) 　※社務所の業務対応時間(＝入店可能時間)は公式ウェブページに詳述 '''靜岡縣護國神社'''（'''しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ'''）は[[静岡県]][[静岡市]]葵区に所在する神社である。神紋は「丸に桜」。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]（[[太平洋戦争]]）における静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者7万6千余柱を祀る。 戦時中までは内務省指定護国神社(県社格)であり、国(官)と神社が分離した戦後は新規創設された神社本庁の包括下となり個別の宗教法人認証を受け設立登記、昭和28年5月29日に神社本庁における別表神社に加列(取扱の類別)した。
社務所2階の戦没者遺品館は駐車場に建設移転した新つつじ会館(静岡県遺族会事務局併設)に移設、戦没英霊の遺品や戦時記念品約千点と戦没者遺影(掲出事業篤志協賛)を展示している。 == 沿革 == *明治10年-静岡浅間神社(国幣小社、駿河総社)にて官軍兵士戦没者の招魂祭(含、慰霊)が斎行、同神社の社殿群一隅で祭祀継続 *駿河伊豆浜松の三地域で個別に戦没者招魂祭(含、慰霊)が斎行されていた *明治20年代末、一県一社の招魂社(維新志士死者、戦没者慰霊の社)を建設する機運が高まる *[[1899年|明治32年]][[11月13日]]- 官民一体で共に祭る「共祭招魂社」として静岡市北番町に創建※創立 *[[1939年|昭和14年]][[4月1日]]- 内務省指定「靜岡縣護國神社」と改称 *[[1942年|昭和17年]][[10月8日]]- 旧境内地(静岡市内中心部)の狭隘により現在地を選定して新築移転遷座 *昭和21年3月28日‐ [[宗教法人法]]による[[宗教法人]]として発足 *[[1946年|昭和22年]]- 戦後の世情動静に鑑み「靜霊神社/しずたまじんじゃ」と改称 *[[1952年|昭和27年]]12月- [[宗教法人]]として県知事の認証を受け登記完了※設立 *[[1953年|昭和28年]]-「靜岡縣護國神社」と復称、所属(被包括)の神社本庁にて別表神社に加列 *昭和53年- 旧社務所老朽化と狭小により社務所新築、竣工 *[[1998年|平成10年]][[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]]- 御鎮座百年奉祝大祭を斎行 *平成30年10月22日、23日- 御鎮座百二十年奉祝大祭を斎行 == 主な祭事 == [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』田中忠雄建築事務所設計、平井工業施工『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』[[1952年]]。]] [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[斎藤寿夫 (政治家)|齊藤寿夫]]誌『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[1952年]]。（左）と『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[石川嘉延]]書『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[1995年]]。（右）]] *歳旦祭-[[1月1日]] *元始祭‐[[1月1日|1月3日]] *節分祭-[[2月3日]] *紀元祭-[[2月11日]] *祈年祭-[[2月17日]] *天長祭-[[2月23日]] *静岡県陸軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[2月23日|3月10日]] *昭和祭-[[2月23日|4月29日]] *菖蒲祭-[[5月5日]] *静岡県海軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[5月5日|5月27日]] *御田植祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|6月]]吉日 *夏越大祓式(茅の輪くぐり)-[[6月30日]] *七夕祭-[[6月30日|7月1週目]]吉日 *万灯みたま祭-[[8月13日]]～[[8月15日|15日]] *英霊顕彰祭-[[8月13日|8月]][[8月15日|15日]] *抜穂祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|10月]]吉日 *収穫奉告祭-抜穂祭同日 *霊璽奉安祭(合祀祭)-10月21日 ※合祀ある時のみ *例祭「慰霊大祭」-[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] *明治祭-[[11月3日]] *創立記念祭-[[11月15日|11月13日]] *七五三祭-[[11月15日]] *新嘗祭-[[11月23日]] *御煤払祭-[[12月13日]] *年越大祓式-[[12月31日]] *除夜祭-12月31日 *月始祭-毎月1日 ※元日は歳旦祭 *月次祭-毎月13日 ※11月は創立記念祭 *命日祭-毎月数回、及び祥月命日に随時執行 *各部隊慰霊祭-恒例日 ※公式web参照 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[護国神社]] *[[静岡陸軍墓地]] == 外部リンク == * [https://shizuokagokoku.jp/ 靜岡縣護國神社（オフィシャルサイト）] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 靜岡縣護國神社（静岡県神社庁）] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすおかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:葵区の歴史]] [[Category:葵区の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |name=Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine]] |location=Shizuoka Prefecture, Shizuoka City, Aoi Ward, Yuzuki 366 |deities=76,233 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture |rank=Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Bekkaku Shrine |founded=1899 (Meiji 32) |main_hall=Nagare-zukuri |annual_festival={{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}}, {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} |latitude = 34.9875 |longitude = 138.4070 |ISO = JP-22 |label_position = bottom |map= Japan Shizuoka }} General worship hours: Shrine grounds are open from 6 AM to 5 PM (6 PM in spring and summer)
* After closing hours, worship is allowed from a distance ≒ 24 hours operation (due to business profile limitations, this notation is used)
* Office hours (entry possible) are detailed on the official website
'''Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine''' (静岡縣護國神社, Shizuoka-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Shizuoka City|ja|静岡市}}, {{ill|Aoi Ward, Shizuoka|ja|葵区}}. The shrine crest is a circle with cherry blossoms. It enshrines over 76,000 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} to the {{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}} ({{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}).
During the war, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of the Interior (with prefectural rank). After the separation of state and shrine post-war, it was established and registered as a religious corporation under the newly formed {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. On May 29, 1953, it was added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines. The war relics hall on the second floor of the shrine office was relocated to the newly constructed Tsutsuji Hall, which also houses the Shizuoka Prefecture Bereaved Families Association Office, displaying about 1,000 war relics and portraits of the war dead.
== History == *1877 (Meiji 10) - A spirit invocation ceremony for war dead soldiers of the government forces was held at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|ja|静岡浅間神社}} (Kokuhen-sha, Sōsha of Suruga), and ceremonies continued at a corner of the shrine buildings. *Individual spirit invocation ceremonies for war dead were held in the three regions of Suruga, Izu, and Hamamatsu. *In the late 1880s, there was a growing movement to construct a single spirit invocation shrine for the entire prefecture. *November 13, 1899 (Meiji 32) - Founded as a "Shared Spirit Invocation Shrine" with joint public and private support, in Kitabancho, Shizuoka City. *April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" by designation of the Ministry of the Interior. *October 8, 1942 (Shōwa 17) - Relocated to the current site due to the narrowness of the old site in central Shizuoka City. *March 28, 1946 (Shōwa 21) - Established as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act. *1947 (Shōwa 22) - Renamed "Shizutama Shrine" considering post-war conditions. *December 1952 (Shōwa 27) - Registered as a religious corporation with the approval of the prefectural governor. *1953 (Shōwa 28) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" and added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. *1978 (Shōwa 53) - New shrine office built due to the aging and narrowness of the old office. *October 22-23, 1998 (Heisei 10) - Centenary commemorative festival held. *October 22-23, 2018 (Heisei 30) - 120th anniversary commemorative festival held.
== Major Festivals == [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|Memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Designed by Tadashi Tanaka Architecture Office, constructed by Hirai Industry. 'Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref>]] [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|Explanation of the memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Explained by Toshio Saito. 'Explanation of the Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref> (left) and '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument'<ref>Written by Yoshinobu Ishikawa. '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument', 1995.</ref> (right)]] * Saitansai - January 1 * Genshisai - January 3 * Setsubunsai - February 3 * Kigensai - February 11 * Kinensai - February 17 * Tenchousai - February 23 * Shizuoka Prefecture Army War Dead Memorial Service - March 10 * Shōwasai - April 29 * Shōbu Festival - May 5 * Shizuoka Prefecture Navy War Dead Memorial Service - May 27 * Otaue Festival (at Shinsen-den) - June (auspicious day) * Nagoshi Oharae Ceremony (Chinowa Kuguri) - June 30 * Tanabata Festival - Early July (auspicious day) * Mantō Mitama Festival - August 13-15 * Eirei Kenshōsai - August 13-15 * Nukihosai (at Shinsen-den) - October (auspicious day) * Harvest Thanksgiving Festival - Same day as Nukihosai * Reiji Hoan Ceremony (Gōshisai) - October 21 (when applicable) * Annual Festival "Irei Taisai" - October 22-23 * Meijisai - November 3 * Foundation Memorial Festival - November 13 * Shichi-Go-San Festival - November 15 * Niinamesai - November 23 * Oosusuharai Festival - December 13 * Year-End Oharae Ceremony - December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival - December 31 * Tsukishizusai - Monthly on the 1st (except January, when Saitansai is held) * Tsukizusai - Monthly on the 13th (except November, when the Foundation Memorial Festival is held) * Memorial Day Ceremonies - Several times monthly, and on memorial anniversaries as needed * Individual Unit Memorial Services - Held regularly (refer to official website)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} * {{ill|Shizuoka Army Cemetery|ja|静岡陸軍墓地}}
== External Links == * [https://shizuokagokoku.jp/ Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine (Official Site)] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine (Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office)] {{Shinto-navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Bekkaku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Aoi-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Aoi-ku]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=靜岡縣護國神社 |画像=[[画像:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|270px|靜岡縣護國神社]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|59|15|N|138|24|25.1|E}}}} |所在地=[[静岡県]][[静岡市]][[葵区]]柚木366 |祭神=静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者 76,233柱 |社格=内務大臣指定[[護国神社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建=[[明治]]32年（[[1899年]]） |本殿=[[流造]] |例祭=[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 59 | 緯度秒 = 15 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 25.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = JP-22 |ラベル位置 = bottom |地図= Japan Shizuoka }} '''靜岡縣護國神社'''（'''しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ'''）は[[静岡県]][[静岡市]]葵区に所在する神社である。神紋は「丸に桜」。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]（[[太平洋戦争]]）における静岡県出身および所縁の戦没者・殉死者7万6千余柱を祀る。 戦時中までは内務省指定護国神社(県社格)であり、国(官)と神社が分離した戦後は新規創設された神社本庁の包括下となり個別の宗教法人認証を受け設立登記、昭和28年5月29日に神社本庁における別表神社に加列(取扱の類別)した。
社務所2階の戦没者遺品館は駐車場に建設移転した新つつじ会館(静岡県遺族会事務局併設)に移設、戦没英霊の遺品や戦時記念品約千点と戦没者遺影(掲出事業篤志協賛)を展示している。 == 沿革 == *明治10年-静岡浅間神社(国幣小社、駿河総社)にて官軍兵士戦没者の招魂祭(含、慰霊)が斎行、同神社の社殿群一隅で祭祀継続 *駿河伊豆浜松の三地域で個別に戦没者招魂祭(含、慰霊)が斎行されていた *明治20年代末、一県一社の招魂社(維新志士死者、戦没者慰霊の社)を建設する機運が高まる *[[1899年|明治32年]][[11月13日]]- 官民一体で共に祭る「共祭招魂社」として静岡市北番町に創建※創立 *[[1939年|昭和14年]][[4月1日]]- 内務省指定「靜岡縣護國神社」と改称 *[[1942年|昭和17年]][[10月8日]]- 旧境内地(静岡市内中心部)の狭隘により現在地を選定して新築移転遷座 *昭和21年3月28日‐ [[宗教法人法]]による[[宗教法人]]として発足 *[[1946年|昭和22年]]- 戦後の世情動静に鑑み「靜霊神社/しずたまじんじゃ」と改称 *[[1952年|昭和27年]]12月- [[宗教法人]]として県知事の認証を受け登記完了※設立 *[[1953年|昭和28年]]-「靜岡縣護國神社」と復称、所属(被包括)の神社本庁にて別表神社に加列 *昭和53年- 旧社務所老朽化と狭小により社務所新築、竣工 *[[1998年|平成10年]][[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]]- 御鎮座百年奉祝大祭を斎行 *平成30年10月22日、23日- 御鎮座百二十年奉祝大祭を斎行 == 主な祭事 == [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』田中忠雄建築事務所設計、平井工業施工『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標』[[1952年]]。]] [[ファイル:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|境内に建立された『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[斎藤寿夫 (政治家)|齊藤寿夫]]誌『静岡県戦没戦災死者慰霊標説明文』[[1952年]]。（左）と『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[石川嘉延]]書『終戦50周年平和祈念事業改修記念碑』[[1995年]]。（右）]] *歳旦祭-[[1月1日]] *元始祭‐[[1月1日|1月3日]] *節分祭-[[2月3日]] *紀元祭-[[2月11日]] *祈年祭-[[2月17日]] *天長祭-[[2月23日]] *静岡県陸軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[2月23日|3月10日]] *昭和祭-[[2月23日|4月29日]] *菖蒲祭-[[5月5日]] *静岡県海軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[5月5日|5月27日]] *御田植祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|6月]]吉日 *夏越大祓式(茅の輪くぐり)-[[6月30日]] *七夕祭-[[6月30日|7月1週目]]吉日 *万灯みたま祭-[[8月13日]]～[[8月15日|15日]] *英霊顕彰祭-[[8月13日|8月]][[8月15日|15日]] *抜穂祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|10月]]吉日 *収穫奉告祭-抜穂祭同日 *霊璽奉安祭(合祀祭)-10月21日 ※合祀ある時のみ *例祭「慰霊大祭」-[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] *明治祭-[[11月3日]] *創立記念祭-[[11月15日|11月13日]] *七五三祭-[[11月15日]] *新嘗祭-[[11月23日]] *御煤払祭-[[12月13日]] *年越大祓式-[[12月31日]] *除夜祭-12月31日 *月始祭-毎月1日 ※元日は歳旦祭 *月次祭-毎月13日 ※11月は創立記念祭 *命日祭-毎月数回、及び祥月命日に随時執行 *各部隊慰霊祭-恒例日 ※公式web参照 == 開閉時間 == 一般的な参拝は社殿6時開門から17時閉門(春夏季18時閉門)まで ※閉門後は少し離れた位置で参拝可 ≒24時間営業(ビジネスプロフィールはこの表記しかできないため) ※社務所の業務対応時間(＝入店可能時間)は公式ウェブページに詳述 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[護国神社]] *[[静岡陸軍墓地]] == 外部リンク == * [https://shizuokagokoku.jp/ 靜岡縣護國神社（オフィシャルサイト）] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 靜岡縣護國神社（静岡県神社庁）] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすおかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] *静岡県陸軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[2月23日|3月10日]] *昭和祭-[[2月23日|4月29日]] *菖蒲祭-[[5月5日]] *静岡県海軍戦没者慰霊祭-[[5月5日|5月27日]] *御田植祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|6月]]吉日 *夏越大祓式(茅の輪くぐり)-[[6月30日]] *七夕祭-[[6月30日|7月1週目]]吉日 *万灯みたま祭-[[8月13日]]～[[8月15日|15日]] *英霊顕彰祭-[[8月13日|8月]][[8月15日|15日]] *抜穂祭(於、神饌田)-[[6月30日|10月]]吉日 *収穫奉告祭-抜穂祭同日 *霊璽奉安祭(合祀祭)-10月21日 ※合祀ある時のみ *例祭「慰霊大祭」-[[10月22日]]、[[10月23日|23日]] *明治祭-[[11月3日]] *創立記念祭-[[11月15日|11月13日]] *七五三祭-[[11月15日]] *新嘗祭-[[11月23日]] *御煤払祭-[[12月13日]] *年越大祓式-[[12月31日]] *除夜祭-12月31日 *月始祭-毎月1日 ※元日は歳旦祭 *月次祭-毎月13日 ※11月は創立記念祭 *命日祭-毎月数回、及び祥月命日に随時執行 *各部隊慰霊祭-恒例日 ※公式web参照 == 開閉時間 == 一般的な参拝は社殿6時開門から17時閉門(春夏季18時閉門)まで ※閉門後は少し離れた位置で参拝可 ≒24時間営業(ビジネスプロフィールはこの表記しかできないため) ※社務所の業務対応時間(＝入店可能時間)は公式ウェブページに詳述 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[護国神社]] *[[静岡陸軍墓地]] == 外部リンク == * [https://shizuokagokoku.jp/ 靜岡縣護國神社（オフィシャルサイト）] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 靜岡縣護國神社（静岡県神社庁）] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすおかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] {{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |name=Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine]] |location=Shizuoka Prefecture, Shizuoka City, Aoi Ward, Yuzuki 366 |deities=76,233 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture |rank=Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Bekkaku Shrine |founded=1899 (Meiji 32) |main_hall=Nagare-zukuri |annual_festival={{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}}, {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} |latitude = 34.9875 |longitude = 138.4070 |ISO = JP-22 |label_position = bottom |map= Japan Shizuoka }}
'''Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine''' (静岡縣護國神社[[ja:静岡縣護國神社]], Shizuoka-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Shizuoka City|ja|静岡市}}, {{ill|Aoi Ward, Shizuoka|ja|葵区}}. The shrine crest is a circle with cherry blossoms. It enshrines over 76,000 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} to the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}.
During the war, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of the Interior (with prefectural rank). After the separation of state and shrine post-war, it was established and registered as a religious corporation under the newly formed {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. On May 29, 1953, it was added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines. The war relics hall on the second floor of the shrine office was relocated to the newly constructed Tsutsuji Hall, which also houses the Shizuoka Prefecture Bereaved Families Association Office, displaying about 1,000 war relics and portraits of the war dead.
== Hours == General worship hours: Shrine grounds are open from 6 AM to 5 PM (6 PM in spring and summer) * After closing hours, worship is allowed from a distance ≒ 24 hours operation (due to business profile limitations, this notation is used)
* Office hours (entry possible) are detailed on the official website == History == *1877 (Meiji 10) - A spirit invocation ceremony for war dead soldiers of the government forces was held at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|ja|静岡浅間神社}} (Kokuhen-sha, Sōsha of Suruga), and ceremonies continued at a corner of the shrine buildings. *Individual spirit invocation ceremonies for war dead were held in the three regions of Suruga, Izu, and Hamamatsu. *In the late 1880s, there was a growing movement to construct a single spirit invocation shrine for the entire prefecture. *November 13, 1899 (Meiji 32) - Founded as a "Shared Spirit Invocation Shrine" with joint public and private support, in Kitabancho, Shizuoka City. *April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" by designation of the Ministry of the Interior. *October 8, 1942 (Shōwa 17) - Relocated to the current site due to the narrowness of the old site in central Shizuoka City. *March 28, 1946 (Shōwa 21) - Established as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act. *1947 (Shōwa 22) - Renamed "Shizutama Shrine" considering post-war conditions. *December 1952 (Shōwa 27) - Registered as a religious corporation with the approval of the prefectural governor. *1953 (Shōwa 28) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" and added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. *1978 (Shōwa 53) - New shrine office built due to the aging and narrowness of the old office. *October 22-23, 1998 (Heisei 10) - Centenary commemorative festival held. *October 22-23, 2018 (Heisei 30) - 120th anniversary commemorative festival held.
== Major Festivals == [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|Memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Designed by Tadashi Tanaka Architecture Office, constructed by Hirai Industry. 'Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref>]] [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|Explanation of the memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Explained by Toshio Saito. 'Explanation of the Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref> (left) and '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument'<ref>Written by Yoshinobu Ishikawa. '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument', 1995.</ref> (right)]] * Saitansai - January 1 * Genshisai - January 3 * Setsubunsai - February 3 * Kigensai - February 11 * Kinensai - February 17 * Tenchousai - February 23 * Shizuoka Prefecture Army War Dead Memorial Service - March 10 * Shōwasai - April 29 * Shōbu Festival - May 5 * Shizuoka Prefecture Navy War Dead Memorial Service - May 27 * Otaue Festival (at Shinsen-den) - June (auspicious day) * Nagoshi Oharae Ceremony (Chinowa Kuguri) - June 30 * Tanabata Festival - Early July (auspicious day) * Mantō Mitama Festival - August 13-15 * Eirei Kenshōsai - August 13-15 * Nukihosai (at Shinsen-den) - October (auspicious day) * Harvest Thanksgiving Festival - Same day as Nukihosai * Reiji Hoan Ceremony (Gōshisai) - October 21 (when applicable) * Annual Festival "Irei Taisai" - October 22-23 * Meijisai - November 3 * Foundation Memorial Festival - November 13 * Shichi-Go-San Festival - November 15 * Niinamesai - November 23 * Oosusuharai Festival - December 13 * Year-End Oharae Ceremony - December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival - December 31 * Tsukishizusai - Monthly on the 1st (except January, when Saitansai is held) * Tsukizusai - Monthly on the 13th (except November, when the Foundation Memorial Festival is held) * Memorial Day Ceremonies - Several times monthly, and on memorial anniversaries as needed * Individual Unit Memorial Services - Held regularly (refer to official website)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} * {{ill|Shizuoka Army Cemetery|ja|静岡陸軍墓地}} [[Category:Original pages]]
== External Links == * [https://shizuokagokoku.jp/ Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine (Official Site)] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine (Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office)] {{Shinto-navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Aoi-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Aoi-ku]]

* Office hours (entry possible) are detailed on the official website == History == *1877 (Meiji 10) - A spirit invocation ceremony for war dead soldiers of the government forces was held at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|ja|静岡浅間神社}} (Kokuhen-sha, Sōsha of Suruga), and ceremonies continued at a corner of the shrine buildings. *Individual spirit invocation ceremonies for war dead were held in the three regions of Suruga, Izu, and Hamamatsu. *In the late 1880s, there was a growing movement to construct a single spirit invocation shrine for the entire prefecture. *November 13, 1899 (Meiji 32) - Founded as a "Shared Spirit Invocation Shrine" with joint public and private support, in Kitabancho, Shizuoka City. *April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" by designation of the Ministry of the Interior. *October 8, 1942 (Shōwa 17) - Relocated to the current site due to the narrowness of the old site in central Shizuoka City. *March 28, 1946 (Shōwa 21) - Established as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act. *1947 (Shōwa 22) - Renamed "Shizutama Shrine" considering post-war conditions. *December 1952 (Shōwa 27) - Registered as a religious corporation with the approval of the prefectural governor. *1953 (Shōwa 28) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" and added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. *1978 (Shōwa 53) - New shrine office built due to the aging and narrowness of the old office. *October 22-23, 1998 (Heisei 10) - Centenary commemorative festival held. *October 22-23, 2018 (Heisei 30) - 120th anniversary commemorative festival held.

'Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref>]] [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|Explanation of the memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Explained by Toshio Saito. '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument', 1995.</ref> (right)]] * Saitansai - January 1 * Genshisai - January 3 * Setsubunsai - February 3 * Kigensai - February 11 * Kinensai - February 17 * Tenchousai - February 23 * Shizuoka Prefecture Army War Dead Memorial Service - March 10 * Shōwasai - April 29 * Shōbu Festival - May 5 * Shizuoka Prefecture Navy War Dead Memorial Service - May 27 * Otaue Festival (at Shinsen-den) - June (auspicious day) * Nagoshi Oharae Ceremony (Chinowa Kuguri) - June 30 * Tanabata Festival - Early July (auspicious day) * Mantō Mitama Festival - August 13-15 * Eirei Kenshōsai - August 13-15 * Nukihosai (at Shinsen-den) - October (auspicious day) * Harvest Thanksgiving Festival - Same day as Nukihosai * Reiji Hoan Ceremony (Gōshisai) - October 21 (when applicable) * Annual Festival "Irei Taisai" - October 22-23 * Meijisai - November 3 * Foundation Memorial Festival - November 13 * Shichi-Go-San Festival - November 15 * Niinamesai - November 23 * Oosusuharai Festival - December 13 * Year-End Oharae Ceremony - December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival - December 31 * Tsukishizusai - Monthly on the 1st (except January, when Saitansai is held) * Tsukizusai - Monthly on the 13th (except November, when the Foundation Memorial Festival is held) * Memorial Day Ceremonies - Several times monthly, and on memorial anniversaries as needed * Individual Unit Memorial Services - Held regularly (refer to official website)

On May 29, 1953, it was added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines. {{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |name=Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine]] |location=Shizuoka Prefecture, Shizuoka City, Aoi Ward, Yuzuki 366 |deities=76,233 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture |rank=Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Bekkaku Shrine |founded=1899 (Meiji 32) |main_hall=Nagare-zukuri |annual_festival={{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}}, {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} |latitude = 34.9875 |longitude = 138.4070 |ISO = JP-22 |label_position = bottom |map= Japan Shizuoka }}
'''Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine''' (静岡縣護國神社[[ja:静岡縣護國神社]], Shizuoka-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Shizuoka City|ja|静岡市}}, {{ill|Aoi Ward, Shizuoka|ja|葵区}}. The shrine crest is a circle with cherry blossoms. It enshrines over 76,000 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} to the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}.
During the war, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of the Interior (with prefectural rank). After the separation of state and shrine post-war, it was established and registered as a religious corporation under the newly formed {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. On May 29, 1953, it was added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines. The war relics hall on the second floor of the shrine office was relocated to the newly constructed Tsutsuji Hall, which also houses the Shizuoka Prefecture Bereaved Families Association Office, displaying about 1,000 war relics and portraits of the war dead.
== Hours == General worship hours: Shrine grounds are open from 6 AM to 5 PM (6 PM in spring and summer) * After closing hours, worship is allowed from a distance ≒ 24 hours operation (due to business profile limitations, this notation is used)
* Office hours (entry possible) are detailed on the official website == History == *1877 (Meiji 10) - A spirit invocation ceremony for war dead soldiers of the government forces was held at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|ja|静岡浅間神社}} (Kokuhen-sha, Sōsha of Suruga), and ceremonies continued at a corner of the shrine buildings. *Individual spirit invocation ceremonies for war dead were held in the three regions of Suruga, Izu, and Hamamatsu. *In the late 1880s, there was a growing movement to construct a single spirit invocation shrine for the entire prefecture. *November 13, 1899 (Meiji 32) - Founded as a "Shared Spirit Invocation Shrine" with joint public and private support, in Kitabancho, Shizuoka City. *April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" by designation of the Ministry of the Interior. *October 8, 1942 (Shōwa 17) - Relocated to the current site due to the narrowness of the old site in central Shizuoka City. *March 28, 1946 (Shōwa 21) - Established as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act. *1947 (Shōwa 22) - Renamed "Shizutama Shrine" considering post-war conditions. *December 1952 (Shōwa 27) - Registered as a religious corporation with the approval of the prefectural governor. *1953 (Shōwa 28) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" and added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. *1978 (Shōwa 53) - New shrine office built due to the aging and narrowness of the old office. *October 22-23, 1998 (Heisei 10) - Centenary commemorative festival held. *October 22-23, 2018 (Heisei 30) - 120th anniversary commemorative festival held.
== Major Festivals == [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|Memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Designed by Tadashi Tanaka Architecture Office, constructed by Hirai Industry. 'Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref>]] [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|Explanation of the memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Explained by Toshio Saito. 'Explanation of the Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref> (left) and '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument'<ref>Written by Yoshinobu Ishikawa. '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument', 1995.</ref> (right)]] * Saitansai - January 1 * Genshisai - January 3 * Setsubunsai - February 3 * Kigensai - February 11 * Kinensai - February 17 * Tenchousai - February 23 * Shizuoka Prefecture Army War Dead Memorial Service - March 10 * Shōwasai - April 29 * Shōbu Festival - May 5 * Shizuoka Prefecture Navy War Dead Memorial Service - May 27 * Otaue Festival (at Shinsen-den) - June (auspicious day) * Nagoshi Oharae Ceremony (Chinowa Kuguri) - June 30 * Tanabata Festival - Early July (auspicious day) * Mantō Mitama Festival - August 13-15 * Eirei Kenshōsai - August 13-15 * Nukihosai (at Shinsen-den) - October (auspicious day) * Harvest Thanksgiving Festival - Same day as Nukihosai * Reiji Hoan Ceremony (Gōshisai) - October 21 (when applicable) * Annual Festival "Irei Taisai" - October 22-23 * Meijisai - November 3 * Foundation Memorial Festival - November 13 * Shichi-Go-San Festival - November 15 * Niinamesai - November 23 * Oosusuharai Festival - December 13 * Year-End Oharae Ceremony - December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival - December 31 * Tsukishizusai - Monthly on the 1st (except January, when Saitansai is held) * Tsukizusai - Monthly on the 13th (except November, when the Foundation Memorial Festival is held) * Memorial Day Ceremonies - Several times monthly, and on memorial anniversaries as needed * Individual Unit Memorial Services - Held regularly (refer to official website)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} * {{ill|Shizuoka Army Cemetery|ja|静岡陸軍墓地}} [[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine[[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine[[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine[[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] == External Links == * [https://shizuokagokoku.jp/ Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine (Official Site)] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406003 Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine (Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office)] {{Shinto-navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しずおかけんごこくじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Aoi-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Aoi-ku]] [[en:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine]]

* Office hours (entry possible) are detailed on the official website == History == *1877 (Meiji 10) - A spirit invocation ceremony for war dead soldiers of the government forces was held at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|ja|静岡浅間神社}} (Kokuhen-sha, Sōsha of Suruga), and ceremonies continued at a corner of the shrine buildings. *Individual spirit invocation ceremonies for war dead were held in the three regions of Suruga, Izu, and Hamamatsu. *In the late 1880s, there was a growing movement to construct a single spirit invocation shrine for the entire prefecture. *November 13, 1899 (Meiji 32) - founded as a "Shared Spirit Invocation Shrine" with joint public and private support, in Kitabancho, Shizuoka City. *April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" by designation of the Ministry of the Interior. *October 8, 1942 (Shōwa 17) - Relocated to the current site due to the narrowness of the old site in central Shizuoka City. *March 28, 1946 (Shōwa 21) - established as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act. *1947 (Shōwa 22) - Renamed "Shizutama Shrine" considering post-war conditions. *December 1952 (Shōwa 27) - Registered as a religious corporation with the approval of the prefectural governor. *1953 (Shōwa 28) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" and added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. *1978 (Shōwa 53) - New shrine office built due to the aging and narrowness of the old office. *October 22-23, 1998 (Heisei 10) - Centenary commemorative festival held. *October 22-23, 2018 (Heisei 30) - 120th anniversary commemorative festival held.

'Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref>]] [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|Explanation of the memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Explained by Toshio Saito. 'Explanation of the Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref> (left) and '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument'<ref>Written by Yoshinobu Ishikawa. '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument', 1995.</ref> (right)]] * Saitansai - January 1 * Genshisai - January 3 * Setsubunsai - February 3 * Kigensai - February 11 * Kinensai - February 17 * Tenchousai - February 23 * Shizuoka Prefecture Army War Dead Memorial Service - March 10 * Shōwasai - April 29 * Shōbu Festival - May 5 * Shizuoka Prefecture Navy War Dead Memorial Service - May 27 * Otaue Festival (at Shinsen-den) - June (auspicious day) * Nagoshi Oharae Ceremony (Chinowa Kuguri) - June 30 * Tanabata Festival - Early July (auspicious day) * Mantō Mitama Festival - August 13-15 * Eirei Kenshōsai - August 13-15 * Nukihosai (at Shinsen-den) - October (auspicious day) * Harvest Thanksgiving Festival - Same day as Nukihosai * Reiji Hoan Ceremony (Gōshisai) - October 21 (when applicable) * Annual Festival "Irei Taisai" - October 22-23 * Meijisai - November 3 * Foundation Memorial Festival - November 13 * Shichi-Go-San Festival - November 15 * Niinamesai - November 23 * Oosusuharai Festival - December 13 * Year-End Oharae Ceremony - December 31 * New Year's Eve Festival - December 31 * Tsukishizusai - Monthly on the 1st (except January, when Saitansai is held) * Tsukizusai - Monthly on the 13th (except November, when the Foundation Memorial Festival is held) * Memorial Day Ceremonies - Several times monthly, and on memorial anniversaries as needed * Individual Unit Memorial Services - Held regularly (refer to official website)

'''Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine''' (静岡縣護國神社[[ja:静岡縣護國神社]], Shizuoka-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Shizuoka City|ja|静岡市}}, {{ill|Aoi Ward, Shizuoka|ja|葵区}}. The shrine crest is a circle with cherry blossoms. It enshrines over 76,000 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} to the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}.
During the war, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of the Interior (with prefectural rank). After the separation of state and shrine post-war, it was established and registered as a religious corporation under the newly formed {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. On May 29, 1953, it was added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines. The war relics hall on the second floor of the shrine office was relocated to the newly constructed Tsutsuji Hall, which also houses the Shizuoka Prefecture Bereaved Families Association Office, displaying about 1,000 war relics and portraits of the war dead.
== Hours == General worship hours: Shrine grounds are open from 6 AM to 5 PM (6 PM in spring and summer) * After closing hours, worship is allowed from a distance ≒ 24 hours operation (due to business profile limitations, this notation is used)
* Office hours (entry possible) are detailed on the official website == History == *1877 (Meiji 10) - A spirit invocation ceremony for war dead soldiers of the government forces was held at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|ja|静岡浅間神社}} (Kokuhen-sha, Sōsha of Suruga), and ceremonies continued at a corner of the shrine buildings. *Individual spirit invocation ceremonies for war dead were held in the three regions of Suruga, Izu, and Hamamatsu. *In the late 1880s, there was a growing movement to construct a single spirit invocation shrine for the entire prefecture. *November 13, 1899 (Meiji 32) - founded as a "Shared Spirit Invocation Shrine" with joint public and private support, in Kitabancho, Shizuoka City. *April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" by designation of the Ministry of the Interior. *October 8, 1942 (Shōwa 17) - Relocated to the current site due to the narrowness of the old site in central Shizuoka City. *March 28, 1946 (Shōwa 21) - established as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act. *1947 (Shōwa 22) - Renamed "Shizutama Shrine" considering post-war conditions. *December 1952 (Shōwa 27) - Registered as a religious corporation with the approval of the prefectural governor. *1953 (Shōwa 28) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" and added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. *1978 (Shōwa 53) - New shrine office built due to the aging and narrowness of the old office. *October 22-23, 1998 (Heisei 10) - Centenary commemorative festival held. *October 22-23, 2018 (Heisei 30) - 120th anniversary commemorative festival held.
== Major festivals == [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|Memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Designed by Tadashi Tanaka Architecture Office, constructed by Hirai Industry. 'Explanation of the Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref> (left) and '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument'<ref>Written by Yoshinobu Ishikawa. '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument', 1995.</ref> (right)]] * Saitansai - January 1 * Genshisai - January 3 * Setsubunsai - February 3 * Kigensai - February 11 * Kinensai - February 17 * Tenchousai - February 23 * Shizuoka Prefecture Army War Dead Memorial Service - March 10 * Shōwasai - April 29 * Shōbu festival - May 5 * Shizuoka Prefecture Navy War Dead Memorial Service - May 27 * Otaue festival (at Shinsen-den) - June (auspicious day) * Nagoshi Oharae ceremony (Chinowa Kuguri) - June 30 * Tanabata festival - Early July (auspicious day) * Mantō Mitama festival - August 13-15 * Eirei Kenshōsai - August 13-15 * Nukihosai (at Shinsen-den) - October (auspicious day) * Harvest Thanksgiving festival - Same day as Nukihosai * Reiji Hoan ceremony (Gōshisai) - October 21 (when applicable) * annual festival "Irei Taisai" - October 22-23 * Meijisai - November 3 * Foundation Memorial festival - November 13 * Shichi-Go-San festival - November 15 * Niinamesai - November 23 * Oosusuharai festival - December 13 * Year-End Oharae ceremony - December 31 * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 * Tsukishizusai - Monthly on the 1st (except January, when Saitansai is held) * Tsukizusai - Monthly on the 13th (except November, when the Foundation Memorial festival is held) * Memorial Day Ceremonies - Several times monthly, and on memorial anniversaries as needed * Individual Unit Memorial Services - Held regularly (refer to official website)

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |name=Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine]] |location=Shizuoka Prefecture, Shizuoka City, Aoi Ward, Yuzuki 366 |deities=76,233 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture |rank=Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Bekkaku Shrine |founded=1899 (Meiji 32) |main_hall=Nagare-zukuri |annual_festival={{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}}, {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} |latitude = 34.9875 |longitude = 138.4070 |ISO = JP-22 |label_position = bottom |map= Japan Shizuoka }} [[ja:靜岡縣護國神社]]
'''Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine''' (静岡縣護國神社[[ja:静岡縣護國神社]], Shizuoka-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Shizuoka City|ja|静岡市}}, {{ill|Aoi Ward, Shizuoka|ja|葵区}}. The shrine crest is a circle with cherry blossoms. It enshrines over 76,000 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} to the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}.
During the war, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of the Interior (with prefectural rank). After the separation of state and shrine post-war, it was established and registered as a religious corporation under the newly formed {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. On May 29, 1953, it was added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines. The war relics hall on the second floor of the shrine office was relocated to the newly constructed Tsutsuji Hall, which also houses the Shizuoka Prefecture Bereaved Families Association Office, displaying about 1,000 war relics and portraits of the war dead.
== Hours == General worship hours: Shrine grounds are open from 6 AM to 5 PM (6 PM in spring and summer) * After closing hours, worship is allowed from a distance ≒ 24 hours operation (due to business profile limitations, this notation is used)
* Office hours (entry possible) are detailed on the official website == History == *1877 (Meiji 10) - A spirit invocation ceremony for war dead soldiers of the government forces was held at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|ja|静岡浅間神社}} (Kokuhen-sha, Sōsha of Suruga), and ceremonies continued at a corner of the shrine buildings. *Individual spirit invocation ceremonies for war dead were held in the three regions of Suruga, Izu, and Hamamatsu. *In the late 1880s, there was a growing movement to construct a single spirit invocation shrine for the entire prefecture. *November 13, 1899 (Meiji 32) - founded as a "Shared Spirit Invocation Shrine" with joint public and private support, in Kitabancho, Shizuoka City. *April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" by designation of the Ministry of the Interior. *October 8, 1942 (Shōwa 17) - Relocated to the current site due to the narrowness of the old site in central Shizuoka City. *March 28, 1946 (Shōwa 21) - established as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act. *1947 (Shōwa 22) - Renamed "Shizutama Shrine" considering post-war conditions. *December 1952 (Shōwa 27) - Registered as a religious corporation with the approval of the prefectural governor. *1953 (Shōwa 28) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" and added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. *1978 (Shōwa 53) - New shrine office built due to the aging and narrowness of the old office. *October 22-23, 1998 (Heisei 10) - Centenary commemorative festival held. *October 22-23, 2018 (Heisei 30) - 120th anniversary commemorative festival held.
== Major festivals == [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|Memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Designed by Tadashi Tanaka Architecture Office, constructed by Hirai Industry. 'Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref>]] [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo Setsumeibun 19521103 and Shusen 50 shunen Heiwa Kinen Jigyo Kaishu Kinenhi 199504.jpg|thumb|200px|Explanation of the memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Explained by Toshio Saito. 'Explanation of the Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref> (left) and '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument'<ref>Written by Yoshinobu Ishikawa. '50th Anniversary of the End of the War Peace Commemoration Project Restoration Monument', 1995.</ref> (right)]] * Saitansai - January 1 * Genshisai - January 3 * Setsubunsai - February 3 * Kigensai - February 11 * Kinensai - February 17 * Tenchousai - February 23 * Shizuoka Prefecture Army War Dead Memorial Service - March 10 * Shōwasai - April 29 * Shōbu festival - May 5 * Shizuoka Prefecture Navy War Dead Memorial Service - May 27 * Otaue festival (at Shinsen-den) - June (auspicious day) * Nagoshi Oharae ceremony (Chinowa Kuguri) - June 30 * Tanabata festival - Early July (auspicious day) * Mantō Mitama festival - August 13-15 * Eirei Kenshōsai - August 13-15 * Nukihosai (at Shinsen-den) - October (auspicious day) * Harvest Thanksgiving festival - Same day as Nukihosai * Reiji Hoan ceremony (Gōshisai) - October 21 (when applicable) * annual festival "Irei Taisai" - October 22-23 * Meijisai - November 3 * Foundation Memorial festival - November 13 * Shichi-Go-San festival - November 15 * Niinamesai - November 23 * Oosusuharai festival - December 13 * Year-End Oharae ceremony - December 31 * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 * Tsukishizusai - Monthly on the 1st (except January, when Saitansai is held) * Tsukizusai - Monthly on the 13th (except November, when the Foundation Memorial festival is held) * Memorial Day ceremony - Several times monthly, and on memorial anniversaries as needed * Individual Unit Memorial Services - Held regularly (refer to official website)

== Major festivals == [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|Memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Designed by Tadashi Tanaka Architecture Office, constructed by Hirai Industry. 'Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref>]] * Saitansai - January 1 * Genshisai - January 3 * Setsubunsai - February 3 * Kigensai - February 11 * Kinensai - February 17 * Tenchousai - February 23 * Shizuoka Prefecture Army War Dead Memorial Service - March 10 * Shōwasai - April 29 * Shōbu festival - May 5 * Shizuoka Prefecture Navy War Dead Memorial Service - May 27 * Otaue festival (at Shinsen-den) - June (auspicious day) * Nagoshi Oharae ceremony (Chinowa Kuguri) - June 30 * Tanabata festival - Early July (auspicious day) * Mantō Mitama festival - August 13-15 * Eirei Kenshōsai - August 13-15 * Nukihosai (at Shinsen-den) - October (auspicious day) * Harvest Thanksgiving festival - Same day as Nukihosai * Reiji Hoan ceremony (Gōshisai) - October 21 (when applicable) * annual festival "Irei Taisai" - October 22-23 * Meijisai - November 3 * Foundation Memorial festival - November 13 * Shichi-Go-San festival - November 15 * Niinamesai - November 23 * Oosusuharai festival - December 13 * Year-End Oharae ceremony - December 31 * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 * Tsukishizusai - Monthly on the 1st (except January, when Saitansai is held) * Tsukizusai - Monthly on the 13th (except November, when the Foundation Memorial festival is held) * Memorial Day ceremony - Several times monthly, and on memorial anniversaries as needed * Individual Unit Memorial Services - Held regularly (refer to official website)

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px|Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine]] |location=Shizuoka Prefecture, Shizuoka City, Aoi Ward, Yuzuki 366 |deities=76,233 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture |rank=Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Bekkaku Shrine |founded=1899 (Meiji 32) |main_hall=Nagare-zukuri |annual_festival={{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}}, {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} |latitude = 34.9875 |longitude = 138.4070 |ISO = JP-22 |label_position = bottom |map= Japan Shizuoka }} [[ja:靜岡縣護國神社]]
'''Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine''' (静岡縣護國神社[[ja:静岡縣護國神社]], Shizuoka-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Shizuoka City|ja|静岡市}}, {{ill|Aoi Ward, Shizuoka|ja|葵区}}. The shrine crest is a circle with cherry blossoms. It enshrines over 76,000 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} to the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}.
During the war, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of the Interior (with prefectural rank). After the separation of state and shrine post-war, it was established and registered as a religious corporation under the newly formed {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. On May 29, 1953, it was added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines. The war relics hall on the second floor of the shrine office was relocated to the newly constructed Tsutsuji Hall, which also houses the Shizuoka Prefecture Bereaved Families Association Office, displaying about 1,000 war relics and portraits of the war dead.
== Hours == General worship hours: Shrine grounds are open from 6 AM to 5 PM (6 PM in spring and summer) * After closing hours, worship is allowed from a distance ≒ 24 hours operation (due to business profile limitations, this notation is used)
* Office hours (entry possible) are detailed on the official website == History == *1877 (Meiji 10) - A spirit invocation ceremony for war dead soldiers of the government forces was held at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|ja|静岡浅間神社}} (Kokuhen-sha, Sōsha of Suruga), and ceremonies continued at a corner of the shrine buildings. *Individual spirit invocation ceremonies for war dead were held in the three regions of Suruga, Izu, and Hamamatsu. *In the late 1880s, there was a growing movement to construct a single spirit invocation shrine for the entire prefecture. *November 13, 1899 (Meiji 32) - founded as a "Shared Spirit Invocation Shrine" with joint public and private support, in Kitabancho, Shizuoka City. *April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" by designation of the Ministry of the Interior. *October 8, 1942 (Shōwa 17) - Relocated to the current site due to the narrowness of the old site in central Shizuoka City. *March 28, 1946 (Shōwa 21) - established as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act. *1947 (Shōwa 22) - Renamed "Shizutama Shrine" considering post-war conditions. *December 1952 (Shōwa 27) - Registered as a religious corporation with the approval of the prefectural governor. *1953 (Shōwa 28) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" and added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. *1978 (Shōwa 53) - New shrine office built due to the aging and narrowness of the old office. *October 22-23, 1998 (Heisei 10) - Centenary commemorative festival held. *October 22-23, 2018 (Heisei 30) - 120th anniversary commemorative festival held.
== Major festivals == [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|Memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Designed by Tadashi Tanaka Architecture Office, constructed by Hirai Industry. * Office hours (entry possible) are detailed on the official website == History == *1877 (Meiji 10) - A spirit invocation ceremony for war dead soldiers of the government forces was held at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|ja|静岡浅間神社}} (Kokuhen-sha, Sōsha of Suruga), and ceremonies continued at a corner of the shrine buildings. *Individual spirit invocation ceremonies for war dead were held in the three regions of Suruga, Izu, and Hamamatsu. *In the late 1880s, there was a growing movement to construct a single spirit invocation shrine for the entire prefecture. *November 13, 1899 (Meiji 32) - founded as a "Shared Spirit Invocation Shrine" with joint public and private support, in Kitabancho, Shizuoka City. *April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" by designation of the Ministry of the Interior. *October 8, 1942 (Shōwa 17) - Relocated to the current site due to the narrowness of the old site in central Shizuoka City. *March 28, 1946 (Shōwa 21) - established as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act. *1947 (Shōwa 22) - Renamed "Shizutama Shrine" considering post-war conditions. *December 1952 (Shōwa 27) - Registered as a religious corporation with the approval of the prefectural governor. *1953 (Shōwa 28) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" and added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. *1978 (Shōwa 53) - New shrine office built due to the aging and narrowness of the old office. *October 22-23, 1998 (Heisei 10) - Centenary commemorative festival held. *October 22-23, 2018 (Heisei 30) - 120th anniversary commemorative festival held.
== Major festivals == [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|Memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Designed by Tadashi Tanaka Architecture Office, constructed by Hirai Industry. 'Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref>]] * Saitansai - January 1 * Genshisai - January 3 * Setsubunsai - February 3 * Kigensai - February 11 * Kinensai - February 17 * Tenchousai - February 23 * Shizuoka Prefecture Army War Dead Memorial Service - March 10 * Shōwasai - April 29 * Shōbu festival - May 5 * Shizuoka Prefecture Navy War Dead Memorial Service - May 27 * Otaue festival (at Shinsen-den) - June (auspicious day) * Nagoshi Oharae ceremony (Chinowa Kuguri) - June 30 * Tanabata festival - Early July (auspicious day) * Mantō Mitama festival - August 13-15 * Eirei Kenshōsai - August 13-15 * Nukihosai (at Shinsen-den) - October (auspicious day) * Harvest Thanksgiving festival - Same day as Nukihosai * Reiji Hoan ceremony (Gōshisai) - October 21 (when applicable) * annual festival "Irei Taisai" - October 22-23 * Meijisai - November 3 * Foundation Memorial festival - November 13 * Shichi-Go-San festival - November 15 * Niinamesai - November 23 * Oosusuharai festival - December 13 * Year-End Oharae ceremony - December 31 * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 * Tsukishizusai - Monthly on the 1st (except January, when Saitansai is held) * Tsukizusai - Monthly on the 13th (except November, when the Foundation Memorial festival is held) * Memorial Day ceremony - Several times monthly, and on memorial anniversaries as needed * Individual Unit Memorial Services - Held regularly (refer to official website)

On May 29, 1953, it was added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines. The war relics hall on the second floor of the shrine office was relocated to the newly constructed Tsutsuji Hall, which also houses the Shizuoka Prefecture Bereaved Families Association Office, displaying about 1,000 war relics and portraits of the war dead.
== Hours == General worship hours: Shrine grounds are open from 6 AM to 5 PM (6 PM in spring and summer) * After closing hours, worship is allowed from a distance ≒ 24 hours operation (due to business profile limitations, this notation is used)
* Office hours (entry possible) are detailed on the official website == History == *1877 (Meiji 10) - A spirit invocation ceremony for war dead soldiers of the government forces was held at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|ja|静岡浅間神社}} (Kokuhen-sha, Sōsha of Suruga), and ceremonies continued at a corner of the shrine buildings. *Individual spirit invocation ceremonies for war dead were held in the three regions of Suruga, Izu, and Hamamatsu. *In the late 1880s, there was a growing movement to construct a single spirit invocation shrine for the entire prefecture. *November 13, 1899 (Meiji 32) - founded as a "Shared Spirit Invocation Shrine" with joint public and private support, in Kitabancho, Shizuoka City. *April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" by designation of the Ministry of the Interior. *October 8, 1942 (Shōwa 17) - Relocated to the current site due to the narrowness of the old site in central Shizuoka City. *March 28, 1946 (Shōwa 21) - established as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act. *1947 (Shōwa 22) - Renamed "Shizutama Shrine" considering post-war conditions. *December 1952 (Shōwa 27) - Registered as a religious corporation with the approval of the prefectural governor. *1953 (Shōwa 28) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" and added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. *1978 (Shōwa 53) - New shrine office built due to the aging and narrowness of the old office. *October 22-23, 1998 (Heisei 10) - Centenary commemorative festival held. *October 22-23, 2018 (Heisei 30) - 120th anniversary commemorative festival held.
== Major festivals == [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|Memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Designed by Tadashi Tanaka Architecture Office, constructed by Hirai Industry. 'Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref>]] * Saitansai - January 1 * Genshisai - January 3 * Setsubunsai - February 3 * Kigensai - February 11 * Kinensai - February 17 * Tenchousai - February 23 * Shizuoka Prefecture Army War Dead Memorial Service - March 10 * Shōwasai - April 29 * Shōbu festival - May 5 * Shizuoka Prefecture Navy War Dead Memorial Service - May 27 * Otaue festival (at Shinsen-den) - June (auspicious day) * Nagoshi Oharae ceremony (Chinowa Kuguri) - June 30 * Tanabata festival - Early July (auspicious day) * Mantō Mitama festival - August 13-15 * Eirei Kenshōsai - August 13-15 * Nukihosai (at Shinsen-den) - October (auspicious day) * Harvest Thanksgiving festival - Same day as Nukihosai * Reiji Hoan ceremony (Gōshisai) - October 21 (when applicable) * festival "Irei Taisai" - October 22-23 * Meijisai - November 3 * Foundation Memorial festival - November 13 * Shichi-Go-San festival - November 15 * Niinamesai - November 23 * Oosusuharai festival - December 13 * Year-End Oharae ceremony - December 31 * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 * Tsukishizusai - Monthly on the 1st (except January, when Saitansai is held) * Tsukizusai - Monthly on the 13th (except November, when the Foundation Memorial festival is held) * Memorial Day ceremony - Several times monthly, and on memorial anniversaries as needed * Individual Unit Memorial Services - Held regularly (refer to official website)

* Office hours (entry possible) are detailed on the official website == History == *1877 (Meiji 10) - A spirit invocation ceremony for war dead soldiers of the government forces was held at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|ja|静岡浅間神社}} (Kokuhen-sha, Sōsha of Suruga), and ceremonies continued at a corner of the shrine buildings. *Individual spirit invocation ceremonies for war dead were held in the three regions of Suruga, Izu, and Hamamatsu. *In the late 1880s, there was a growing movement to construct a single spirit invocation shrine for the entire prefecture. *November 13, 1899 (Meiji 32) - founded as a "Shared Spirit Invocation Shrine" with joint public and private support, in Kitabancho, Shizuoka City. *April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" by designation of the Ministry of the Interior. *October 8, 1942 (Shōwa 17) - Relocated to the current site due to the narrowness of the old site in central Shizuoka City. *March 28, 1946 (Shōwa 21) - established as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act. *1947 (Shōwa 22) - Renamed "Shizutama Shrine" considering post-war conditions. *December 1952 (Shōwa 27) - Registered as a religious corporation with the approval of the prefectural governor. *1953 (Shōwa 28) - Renamed "Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine" and added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. *1978 (Shōwa 53) - New shrine office built due to the aging and narrowness of the old office. *October 22-23, 1998 (Heisei 10) - Centenary commemorative festival held. *October 22-23, 2018 (Heisei 30) - 120th anniversary commemorative festival held.
== Major festivals == [[File:Shizuoka ken Senbotsu Sensaishisha Ireihyo 19521103.jpg|thumb|200px|Memorial monument for the war dead and war victims of Shizuoka Prefecture<ref>Designed by Tadashi Tanaka Architecture Office, constructed by Hirai Industry. 'Memorial Monument for the War Dead and War Victims of Shizuoka Prefecture', 1952.</ref>]] * Saitansai - January 1 * Genshisai - January 3 * Setsubunsai - February 3 * Kigensai - February 11 * Kinensai - February 17 * Tenchousai - February 23 * Shizuoka Prefecture Army War Dead Memorial Service - March 10 * Shōwasai - April 29 * Shōbu festival - May 5 * Shizuoka Prefecture Navy War Dead Memorial Service - May 27 * Otaue festival (at Shinsen-den) - June (auspicious day) * Nagoshi Oharae ceremony (Chinowa Kuguri) - June 30 * Tanabata festival - Early July (auspicious day) * Mantō Mitama festival - August 13-15 * Eirei Kenshōsai - August 13-15 * Nukihosai (at Shinsen-den) - October (auspicious day) * Harvest Thanksgiving festival - Same day as Nukihosai * Reiji Hoan ceremony (Gōshisai) - October 21 (when applicable) * festival "Irei Taisai" - October 22-23 * Meijisai - November 3 * Foundation Memorial festival - November 13 * Shichi-Go-San festival - November 15 * Niinamesai - November 23 * Oosusuharai festival - December 13 * Year-End Oharae ceremony - December 31 * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 * Tsukishizusai - Monthly on the 1st (except January, when Saitansai is held) * Tsukizusai - Monthly on the 13th (except November, when the Foundation Memorial festival is held) * Memorial Day ceremony - Several times monthly, and on memorial anniversaries as needed * Individual Unit Memorial Services - Held regularly (refer to official website)

'''Shizuoka Gokoku Shrine''' (静岡縣護國神社[[ja:静岡縣護國神社]], Shizuoka-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Shizuoka City|ja|静岡市}}, {{ill|Aoi Ward, Shizuoka|ja|葵区}}. The shrine crest is a circle with cherry blossoms. It enshrines over 76,000 war dead and martyrs from Shizuoka Prefecture from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} to the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}.
During the war, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of the Interior (with prefectural rank). After the separation of state and shrine post-war, it was established and registered as a religious corporation under the newly formed {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. On May 29, 1953, it was added to the list of Bekkaku Shrines. The war relics hall on the second floor of the shrine office was relocated to the newly constructed Tsutsuji Hall, which also houses the Shizuoka Prefecture Bereaved Families Association Office, displaying about 1,000 war relics and portraits of the war dead.
== Hours == General worship hours: Shrine grounds are open from 6 AM to 5 PM (6 PM in spring and summer) * After closing hours, worship is allowed from a distance ≒ 24 hours operation (due to business profile limitations, this notation is used)
